key_points: Private bathrooms common; dorms rated highly by current students
UV apartment-style housing praised but expensive, eats full tuition
Marietta campus dorm options unclear; students ask if modern dorms exist there

she's probably gonna have to use all of her tuition to live at UV

key_points: Commuter-heavy culture makes socializing harder, especially Marietta campus
Marietta campus described as 'socially dead' by multiple students
Kennesaw town offers easy Atlanta access; mountains nearby for weekends
key_quotes: The Marietta campus is allegedly socially dead
Kennesaw is a cool town. It's far enough outside ATL that it's not too crazy
I want to have fun in college, go out, parties, events

key_points: Food consistently praised as a standout positive by students
Multiple Reddit commenters voluntarily mention food quality unprompted
No serious complaints about dining found in either source
key_quotes: the food is bomb. have fun!
My brother goes to KSU and he enjoys it. Getting better and great food lol
Not related to the app, but the food is bomb

key_points: No dedicated mental health support discussion found in data
Students mention stress from transfer anxiety and financial pressure
One student referenced a near-death experience on campus with emotional attachment
key_quotes: I didn't like KSU at all. I don't like talking about it because I don't want to relive those moments
Consistent struggles with mental illness which incredibly impacted my high-school grades
I was so scared one night... Kennesaw state saved my life

key_points: HOPE and Zell Miller scholarships make KSU extremely affordable for Georgia residents
Total 4-year cost around $74k even with HOPE; manageable but not trivial
Financial aid bureaucracy causes real problems; one student's mom withheld SS card to block scholarship
key_quotes: $74,000 that I nor my parents have. That's four years total. I'm going to KSU and that price is WITH the HOPE
Important information for getting scholarships is being withheld from me
my GSAL loan went over the cost of attendance... they're saying if I don't give them the thousand by the 12th they'll send it to collections

key_points: Polytechnic/Marietta campus hit hard by part-time professors who don't know material
CS program considered best among KSU/GSU/Georgia Southern trio by students
Computer Engineering Tech degree may not qualify for FE/PE engineering exams
key_quotes: They decided to charge full state tuition, jumped people in required classes, then chose all part-time professors who did not know the material
I believe that KSU has the best program of the three, as UGA's is very new and KSU absorbed Southern Poly
No. Techs are not considered engineers and possibly cannot qualify for the FE/PE exams

key_points: Counselors at Polytechnic campus only available by email; students felt abandoned
Admissions portal has technical errors blocking applicants from submitting
Loan disputes handled aggressively; research stipend caused collections threat
key_quotes: Counselors were only available by email
my email does not exist in tenant 'Kennesaw State University' and cannot access the application
Their Dean was recently fired after bribery to the staff. Now they're having issues

key_points: Split campuses create confusion; Marietta is STEM-focused but socially isolated
Kennesaw main campus walkable and visually appealing per tour videos
Atlanta proximity is a genuine asset for internships, nightlife, and networking
key_quotes: The Marietta campus is allegedly socially dead. Not a bad place to try to transfer into Tech from
Kennesaw is a cool town. Some awesome small towns and mountains are just up 75
because of its size and proximity to Atlanta you'll probably have better networking

key_points: Seen primarily as a transfer launchpad to Georgia Tech, not a career destination
KSU-to-GT pipeline exists and is actively used by engineering students
Game Design grad from Marietta campus reports positive experience; seeks industry work
key_quotes: the KSU/GSU pipeline to GT is your best bet
I graduate May 2023 for Computer Game Design and Development... I've enjoyed my time here so far. Marietta Campus
I believe that KSU has the best program of the three

key_points: Extremely affordable for Georgia residents with HOPE/Zell Miller; often under $5k tuition
International and OOS students pay ~$18k/year with limited scholarship options
Nursing students specifically cite KSU as financially smart choice
key_quotes: Their tuition is about $5k per year... with my family income it would be around 18k
If I go to KSU I'll be commuting from home and will pay 3k out of pocket
KSU is the better option. The extra 4,000 per year for UGA isn't worth the higher ranking

key_points: Many students attend as a stepping stone, not a destination school
Enthusiastic minority of students genuinely love it; transfer-minded majority coexist
Athletic and social culture less dominant than UGA; more academically transactional
key_quotes: I came here for a field trip when I was little and now I'm still at KSU and it is amazing
KSU is slept on imo. If you're staying a year and transferring, you picked a good place to do it
I genuinely feel like I wasted all these years to end up at a shitty state school. I'm going to Kennesaw State University now
